errors: [ELM exclusion for LDL-C uses `> 190 mg/dL` instead of the CPG-required `>= 190 mg/dL`; ELM adds non-CPG exclusion criteria (end-stage renal disease, recent ESRD encounter, dialysis/dependence on dialysis, cirrhosis, rhabdomyolysis) that are not part of the USPSTF recommendation; ELM references undefined/placeholder value sets (`"?"`) for smoking status, CVD risk assessment, pregnancy observation, and breastfeeding observation, which does not explicitly match the CPG logic]

The ELM error logic does not exactly match the CPG requirement. The CPG specifies a single error message when screening is not needed (`InPopulation = false`), but the ELM implementation adds an additional alternate error (`"Unable to generate weight screening recommendation. Please verify patient data is complete or contact support."`) in the `ELSE` branch. All numeric thresholds and the 1-month lookback match exactly.

Missing required qualifying encounter criterion from the CPG (“Encounter to Screen for Depression” value set `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916`) in the ELM inclusion logic. The ELM defines `In Demographic` and `Needs Depression Screening` without any encounter-based inclusion check, so the logical structure does not fully implement the CPG decision logic.

; `Has Recent DEXA Scan` does not fully match the CPG: the guideline requires a DEXA scan performed within the past **2 years **with a **result present**, but the ELM only checks `S.issued IN [Today - 2 years to Today]` and does not verify that a result is present.; `Has Osteoporotic Fracture` does not correctly implement the exclusion criterion: the CPG excludes patients with a **history of osteoporotic fractures** from the specified value set, but the ELM checks only for `NOT (IsNull(C.clinicalStatus))`, which is not equivalent to identifying a fracture diagnosis/history from the value set.

Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age >= 18 years, but ELM uses age >= 25 years. Time interval mismatch: CPG requires a 12-month lookback (past 1 year), but ELM uses 6 months for Encounter to Screen for Blood Pressure.
Age upper threshold mismatch: CPG requires age <= 85 years, but ELM implementation uses age <= 80 years.
HbA1c Laboratory Test threshold mismatch: CPG requires most recent HbA1c >= 6.5%, but ELM implementation uses >= 9 g%.

[ELM "Has Recent Pap Smear" checks only for a Pap Test Observation issued within the past 3 years, but the CPG requires a Pap Test within the past 3 years **with a result present**; the result-present condition is missing]

ELM Smoking History logic does not fully match the CPG inclusion criteria: it only checks for a Condition in the Tobacco User value set with `clinicalStatus = active`, but the CPG requires an active smoker or former smoker with **at least 20 pack-years** who **quit within the past 15 years**. The pack-year and quit-within-15-years requirements are missing.

[Has Smoking History is implemented as a Condition retrieve with clinicalStatus.code in active/resolved, which does not exactly match the CPG requirement of “ever smoked (100+ cigarettes in lifetime)” from the Tobacco User value set; Has Prior AAA Screening is implemented as an Observation retrieve with non-null value, but the CPG requires a prior abdominal aortic aneurysm ultrasound screening from the AAA Screening Ultrasound value set to have ever been performed; the CPG requires screening to be excluded if prior AAA ultrasound has ever been performed, but the ELM logic only checks for any observation value and does not explicitly verify the screening event/value set semantics.]

Rationale text does not fully match the CPG: the CPG specifies that USPSTF recommends screening and providing brief behavioral counseling interventions, and includes the Grade B recommendation wording, but the ELM rationale omits these elements.

Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age 45 to 75 years, but ELM uses 60 to 75 years. Time interval mismatch: CPG requires no colorectal cancer screening within the past 1 year, but ELM checks for screening within the past 2 years.

Time interval mismatch: CPG requires no screening if a Generalized Anxiety Disorder Screening has been performed within the past **1 year**, but the ELM uses a **6 month** lookback period. Recommendation output mismatch: when InPopulation is true, the CPG requires a non-null rationale ("USPSTF recommends screening..."), but the ELM sets rationale to null.

Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires inclusion for patients aged 15 to 65 years, but the ELM implementation uses age >= 30 years. The ELM also returns null rationale even when screening is needed, whereas the CPG specifies a non-null rationale string for InPopulation = true.

[BMI threshold mismatch: CPG requires most recent BMI measurement >= 25 kg/m2, but ELM uses BMI >= 35 kg/m2; Lookback period mismatch: CPG requires no glucose/HbA1c test within the past 3 years, but ELM uses 1 year; Temporal logic mismatch: CPG requires the most recent BMI measurement, but ELM uses EXISTS any BMI observation meeting the threshold; Test-result requirement mismatch: CPG requires a glucose/HbA1c test with a result present, but ELM only checks for test issuance date; Recommendation/rationale mismatch: when InPopulation is true, CPG specifies a non-null rationale/indicator/source, but ELM sets rationale to null and does not include the required additional output fields.]

Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age **21 to 65** years, but ELM uses **age >= 30 and <= 65** years. This excludes eligible patients aged 21–29.; Screening logic mismatch: CPG says screening is not needed only if a **Pap Test has been performed within the past 3 years with a result present**. ELM checks only for a recent Pap smear by **issued date within 3 years** and does not explicitly require a result present.

[Lookback period mismatch: CPG requires no depression screening within the past 1 year, but the ELM implementation uses a 3 month lookback for both adult and adolescent depression screening. This changes the screening eligibility logic and does not match the guideline exactly.]
Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age >= 65 years, but ELM uses age >= 75 years in "In Demographic".
Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age >= 18 years, but ELM implementation uses age >= 25 years in "In Demographic".

[Lookback period mismatch: CPG requires no DEXA scan performed within the past 2 years, but ELM uses 6 months; Osteoporotic Fracture exclusion is implemented incorrectly because ELM checks only that clinicalStatus is not null rather than any history of osteoporotic fracture; Osteoporosis/Osteopenia exclusions are limited to active diagnoses in ELM, while the CPG excludes existing diagnoses generally]

[Age threshold mismatch: ELM uses age >= 55 years, but CPG requires age >= 65 years; Smoking history logic mismatch: ELM infers "Has Smoking History" from Condition records with clinicalStatus active/resolved in Tobacco User value set, while CPG requires ever smoked (100+ cigarettes in lifetime) from the Tobacco User value set; Because of the age mismatch, the inclusion population is broader than the CPG; No other numeric/time-interval mismatches identified.]

Logical mismatch in `MeetsInclusionCriteria`: the CPG requires **ALL** criteria to be true using **AND** (`age >= 40` AND `female` AND `no mammogram in past 1 year`), but the ELM implementation uses `([Screening Age] OR [Gender]) AND [Mammogram]`, which incorrectly uses **OR** between age and gender.

ELM implementation mismatch: `Needs Tobacco Use Screening` / `InPopulation` only checks age >= 18 and does not include the required exclusion of **no tobacco use screening performed in the past 1 year with a result present**. The CPG requires **Age >= 18 AND NOT recent tobacco screening within 1 year**, but the ELM logic omits the `NOT EXISTS recent screening` condition.

[ELM implementation is missing the upper age limit of 80 years; CPG requires age 50 to 80 inclusive, but ELM only checks age >= 50. ELM smoking-history logic does not match CPG: it checks for a Tobacco User condition with clinicalStatus.code = active, but CPG requires an active smoker or former smoker with at least 20 pack-years who quit within the past 15 years. ELM does not verify pack-year history or quit-within-15-years criteria. The recent LDCT lookback period of 1 year matches the CPG.]

ELM makes the Interstitial Pulmonary Fibrosis inclusion criterion stricter than the CPG by requiring the condition to be both **Confirmed** and **Active**. The CPG only requires the presence of the condition “Interstitial Pulmonary Fibrosis [Unspecified Cause]” and does not specify confirmed/active modifiers for this inclusion criterion.

Connective Tissue Disorders exclusion logic is inverted in the ELM. The CPG says to exclude patients who DO have a confirmed active connective tissue disorder, but the implementation defines `Connective Tissue Disorders (SNOMED)` as `NOT (EXISTS (ActiveCondition(Confirmed(...))))`, which returns true when no CTD is present and then incorrectly includes that in `MeetsExclusionCriteria`. This reverses the intended exclusion criterion.

[Needs AAA Screening omits the required Male sex criterion from the CPG inclusion criteria; Has Prior AAA Screening is implemented as an Observation with non-null value, which may not exactly match the CPG requirement of any prior AAA screening ultrasound ever performed; Has Smoking History uses Condition clinical status logic (active/resolved) rather than explicitly matching the Tobacco User value set definition from the CPG]

Missing required exclusion and inclusion logic: the CPG requires **no active Bipolar Disorder diagnosis** and **no active Depression diagnosis**, but the ELM `Needs Depression Screening` logic only excludes **Has Depression Diagnosis** and does not exclude **Has Bipolar Diagnosis**. Also, the CPG requires a **qualifying encounter during the measurement period** (`Encounter to Screen for Depression` value set), but the ELM implementation does not include any encounter-based inclusion criterion.

Logical mismatch: the CPG requires **age >= 50 AND no colonoscopy in the past 1 month** (count = 0), but the ELM implements **age >= 50 AND colonoscopy in the past 1 month exists** (`COUNT(...) > 0`). This inverts the inclusion criteria and screening recommendation logic.

[ELM logical structure is inverted: CPG requires Age >= 65 AND NO falls risk assessment in the past 1 year, but ELM defines "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" as Age >= 65 AND HAS Recent Falls Assessment. This incorrectly triggers screening when a recent assessment exists instead of when it is absent.]

[Missing qualifying encounter criterion from "Encounter to Screen for Depression" value set `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916` in the inclusion logic; CPG requires age >= 12 AND a qualifying encounter during measurement period.], [Incorrect exclusion logic: `NOT ([Has Bipolar Diagnosis] AND [Has Depression Diagnosis])` does not match the CPG requirement of excluding patients with active bipolar disorder OR active depression diagnosis; it should be `NOT Has Bipolar Diagnosis AND NOT Has Depression Diagnosis`.]

The exclusion logic is incorrect: the CPG requires **MeetsExclusionCriteria = CTD active OR active amiodarone**, but the ELM implements **MeetsExclusionCriteria = CTD active AND amiodarone**, which would fail to exclude patients who have only one of the two exclusion criteria.

Value set mismatch: CPG requires "Fall Risk Assessment" value set `2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.118.12.1028`, but ELM uses `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.564`. Numeric values and time interval match exactly.
Value set reference mismatch: CPG requires Alcohol Use Screening value set `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542`, but the ELM implementation uses `2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1278`.
